behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before any downstream processing; missing fields return one clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (validator must block insurance/triage/scheduling), not just local field checks. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agentic: none. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is only about pre-check gating and error aggregation before downstream calls.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake orchestrator with real validator + downstream service fakes/spies; submit payload missing multiple required fields; assert one error lists every missing field and assert zero downstream invocations occurred.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is pure validation logic in one boundary. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration+ not needed because behavior text does not require module interaction, only deterministic parsing/range rules.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test DOB validator with table-driven cases (future date, >150 years old, invalid string, valid boundary dates) and assert exact pass/fail outcomes.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending responses correctly.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: request/response contract bugs often appear at real HTTP boundary (serialization, headers, status/body mapping). REAL-INFRA: yes, real HTTP/API behavior is part of the failure boundary. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior explicitly requires correct outbound payload to payer API and real response interpretation at that boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run service against a real test payer endpoint (or HTTP sandbox) and execute three response scenarios; verify outbound patient ID/plan code on wire and resulting internal status mapping.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues instead of blocking.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unreachable conditions (DNS/connect timeout/socket failure) are infrastructure-level and mocks can hide retry/timeout semantics. REAL-INFRA: yes, network failure behavior is central. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text is explicitly about real API unreachability handling.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger real connection failure to payer endpoint (e.g., blackholed host/closed port) and assert pipeline sets eligibility=pending and proceeds to subsequent intake stages.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model judgment quality; stubbed outputs would always pass and hide failures. REAL-INFRA may exist but model-output dependence dominates minimum level. MULTI-STEP: single model decision, not multi-agent pipeline. Rejection gate: Integration/System are disqualified because they can validate plumbing but not real triage decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run real triage model on a curated emergency symptom set and assert high-urgency classification meets threshold/expected labels across repeated runs with acceptance bands for nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: Symptom triage returns a clinician-reviewable natural-language explanation, not only a numeric urgency score.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior depends on quality/content of generated explanation text. Deterministic stubs cannot verify whether explanation is clinically interpretable and aligned to symptoms. MULTI-STEP: single model output requirement. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text targets model-generated explanation quality, not just field presence.
behaviors.test_description: Call real triage model with representative symptom inputs; assert explanation field is present, references key symptoms/rationale, and passes clinician-readable rubric checks.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high=same-day, medium=within 48h).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when urgency output, slot query, and scheduling policy interact. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess boundary in behavior text. LLM-DEP: none (assume urgency input already assigned). MULTI-STEP agentic: none. Rejection gate: System disqualified because local DB/module wiring is sufficient to expose policy bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use real scheduling service + repository on test DB with seeded calendar; submit high/medium urgency requests and assert selected slot is earliest and satisfies SLA windows.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book a slot when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: race condition emerges at module + DB transaction/locking boundary, not in isolated function logic. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with real local DB concurrency semantics; no external OS/network requirement beyond process/db interaction. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agentic: none. Rejection gate: System considered, but behavior text only requires concurrency correctness on shared calendar DB, which a local integration environment can reproduce.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent booking attempts for same slot against test DB with real transaction constraints; assert exactly one succeeds and the other gets conflict handling path.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk is incorrect data handoff from scheduling/provider/visit-type modules into message composer. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS dependency required to catch this. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agentic: none. Rejection gate: Unit disqualified because behavior text requires composed fields from multiple modules, not just one template function input.
behaviors.test_description: Execute post-scheduling confirmation generation through real module wiring with representative visit types; assert all required fields and correct prep instructions appear in final message payload.

behaviors.description: Full intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Behavior traverses real end-to-end path including AI triage call; per LLM-DEP rule, real model invocation on execution path makes mock-only tests insufficient even with deterministic completion assertion. REAL-INFRA also present (payer API/DB), but model-dependent step sets minimum above pure System. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic decision, so Workflow not strictly required by definition. Rejection gate: Workflow considered but disqualified because behavior text does not specify multiple agentic stages, only one triage step within a broader pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake in staging with real payer sandbox, real triage model, and real scheduling DB for one patient payload; assert pipeline reaches confirmation state automatically with no operator intervention.
